text.title: statistics-application Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for statistics-application as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs confidently compute p-values from headline percentages without raw counts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always force n1, x1, n2, x2 as explicit inputs; percentages without sample sizes are meaningless.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No, it is not. Force a binary significant_at_alpha field; there is no "almost significant" in frequentist stats.

failure-mode.description: "95% CI does not cross 0" is misinterpreted as "95% chance B is better".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: That is Bayesian. Frequentist: "If H0 is true, there is a 5% chance of seeing this data." Report CI: "We are 95% confident the true lift is between X% and Y%."

failure-mode.description: Sample-size prompts drop alpha and power assumptions silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sample size is meaningless without alpha and power. Require them in output: alpha=0.05, power=0.80 (or whatever was used).
